The syntax for finding files with a specific modification time is something like find /path/to/directory -mtime +31d -type f. Here, -mtime +31d means files modified more than 31 days ago. The -type f ensures we only get files, not directories.

So the command would be: find /testdata/system -mtime +31d -type f -rmdir. Wait, but -rmdir deletes the directory, not the files. The user wants to remove files, not directories. So maybe the correct command is find -type f -mtime +31d -rmdir. That way, we delete the files recursively.
